iterate_latest.summary: Diagnosis-only iterate. PR #61 (ADR-129) shipped touch-scroll.ts using term.scrollLines(); user-report 2026-06-07 says it does not work during an active Claude session. Root cause confirmed empirically: CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1 default-ON (CLAUDE.md rule 22 / ADR-095) puts Claude TUI into the xterm.js alt-screen buffer (DECSET 1049); alt-buffer has no scrollback, so term.scrollLines() resolves with no viewport motion. PR #61's mock-Terminal pattern (scrollLines: vi.fn()) could not model buffer-type semantics. Added 3 vitest cases in touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts that instantiate a REAL @xterm/xterm Terminal in jsdom (no term.open() — parser runs synchronously on write() with buffer state observable). Tests PASS with the broken code on purpose, documenting the failure mode. No production code touched. Follow-up iterate (to be opened next) inverts the assertions and adds buffer-aware routing.

server.reason: client-only diff (no server/** file touched); touch-scroll is a pure client module

unit.evidence: full client vitest 149 files / 1553 tests green (was 148 / 1550 before split + alt-buffer cases); touch-scroll mock cohort 15 + alt-buffer cohort 3 = 18/18 green; tsc --noEmit clean; oxlint clean on changed files. Test file split: touch-scroll.test.ts 195 → 200 LOC, alt-buffer file 117 LOC — both under the 300-LOC ceiling.

integration.reason: test-only diff; no integration boundary touched

e2e.reason: test-only diff; no UI or transport surface changed

surface_verification.justification: Diagnosis-only iterate: no production code change. Added two real-xterm vitest cases in touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts that empirically reproduce the alt-buffer scrollLines no-op (3 assertions, 18/18 client suite green). No UI surface touched, no API surface touched. Follow-up fix iterate will invert the assertions and add the buffer-aware routing.

behaviors.behavior: EMPIRICAL: real @xterm/xterm Terminal in jsdom, given `\x1b[?1049h`, flips buffer.active.type from 'normal' to 'alternate' (and back on `\x1b[?1049l`)
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'DECSET 1049 flips buffer.active.type to alternate' (uses async writeAsync(term, data) to wait for parser callback; passes 1/1)

behaviors.behavior: EMPIRICAL: term.scrollLines(-10) moves buffer.active.viewportY in the normal buffer (sanity baseline) but produces no motion in the alt buffer (the bug)
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'in the alt-buffer, scrollLines does NOT move viewportY' — proves the inverted control: normal-buffer scrollLines DOES move viewportY (toBeLessThan), alt-buffer scrollLines does NOT (toBe baseline)

behaviors.behavior: STRUCTURAL: attachTouchScroll wired to a real alt-buffer xterm still calls term.scrollLines unconditionally (regardless of buffer type) — this is the defect the follow-up iterate fixes
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'attachTouchScroll wired to a real alt-buffer xterm still calls scrollLines' — vi.spyOn pass-through, asserts spy.toHaveBeenCalled; this assertion INVERTS in the follow-up iterate

behaviors.behavior: REGRESSION: the existing 15 mock-Terminal touch-scroll tests (consumeTouchDelta math + attachTouchScroll integration) still pass after the split + new file
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.test.ts 15/15 green (unchanged surface; mock-Terminal cohort kept intact after the alt-buffer split into touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts)

enumeration_basis.plus: BUG-diagnosis (Spec Impact NONE, no formal ACs) — basis = the 3 alt-buffer behaviors empirically proven plus 1 regression-guarantee that the mock cohort still passes after the file split. All four observable in CI via the vitest suite; no behavior is testable-but-untested.
